title: Threshold Session Reduced to Hill Repeats (7 km Hill Repeat Run)

sections.markdown:
- 12 km run including 5 x 1 km threshold work
- 2 km easy warm-up
- 5 x 1 km at controlled threshold effort with `90 seconds` easy jog between reps
- Cool down easy to `12 km` total
- Keep the first rep disciplined so the fifth rep still looks like threshold running rather than a grind.
- Monday is slightly longer because basketball dropped out, so the goal here is quality off controlled load, not chasing a hero session.

- This stayed on the planned Tuesday date, but it did not stay as the planned `12 km` threshold workout. The recorded run was `7.025 km` in `37:34` moving time (`37:46` elapsed) with `111.4 m` of climbing, average HR `165.2`, and max HR `180`, so the session finished `4.975 km` short of the original prescription.
- The stream data shows a shorter hill-repeat substitute rather than `5 x 1 km` threshold work. After a `1.23 km` downhill-leaning warm-up in about `6:35` at roughly `5:18 /km` and average HR about `149`, the route shifts into repeated uphill efforts.
- Between `1.23 km` and `5.08 km`, the stream shows eight short climbs, each about `0.18 to 0.23 km`, `56 to 75` seconds, and `7.8 to 10.4 m` of gain, with jogging descents of about `0.30 km` between them. There was then a longer uphill push from `5.62 km` to `5.94 km` that covered `0.324 km`, gained `17 m`, and took about `1:42`.
- That means this still counted as a real quality touch, but it was a shorter, more climb-focused session than the planned threshold set. Given that Monday had already drifted to `12.194 km` at average HR `164.6`, cutting the session back was the honest load-management call rather than pretending the week still needed full threshold volume.

- The immediate job is to let this stay as reduced quality, not to chase the missing threshold work on Wednesday or Thursday. The week has already moved from Monday's `12.194 km` to `19.219 km` by the end of Tuesday, and both days carried average heart rate in the mid-`160s`.
- The recovery jogs between the mid-run climbs did not really bring heart rate back down once the session settled in. By the later reps and the longer climb, average HR was sitting around `170 to 174`, which is enough cumulative cost that Wednesday should stay genuinely easy.
- Thursday's steady run should remain steady. The useful short-term win is getting back to aerobic support and keeping the shins quiet, not turning the rest of the week into make-up work for a session that was deliberately reduced.

- This run still helps the block because it preserved a controlled quality signal on the scheduled quality day without forcing a full threshold session on tired legs. That is a better durability decision than stacking two harder days back to back and hoping the week absorbs it.
- The tradeoff is that the block did not bank the planned threshold volume. For the half-marathon build and the later marathon block, the long-term goal is still to complete full threshold sessions when the support days are actually supportive.
- The main coaching read is positive if the week stays disciplined from here: Monday and Tuesday together showed you can handle back-to-back load, but they also showed that durable progression depends on separating reduced-quality days from true threshold days instead of blending them together.
- I ended up doing a shorter hill repeat run instead since we did a harder run yesterday.

- Apple Health recorded `240` during-run heart-rate samples. Average HR was `165.7 bpm`, ranging from `127.0` to `180.0 bpm`.
- First-half average HR was `160.8 bpm` and second-half average HR was `170.5 bpm` (+9.7 bpm), which shows clear late cardiovascular drift.

- Apple Health cadence averaged `177.5 spm`, with a working range of `100.0` to `206.7 spm` after excluding `2` start-stop outliers below `100 spm`.
- First-half cadence averaged `177.6 spm` and second-half cadence averaged `177.4 spm` (-0.3 spm), which looks mechanically stable.

- Strava moving pace averaged `5:21 /km` across `7.025 km` in `37:34` moving time.
- Estimated first-half moving pace was `5:14 /km` and second-half pace was `5:28 /km` (+13 s/km), so pace eased a little late.

- Strava summary HR came in at `165.2 bpm` average and `180 bpm` max.
- Distance-split HR averaged `159.6 bpm` in the first half and `170.6 bpm` in the second (+11.0 bpm), which shows clear late cardiovascular drift.

- Moving time was `37:34` out of `37:46` elapsed (99.5%), which kept stoppage cost negligible.
- The downsampled moving stream shows `5` stop segments and `0.5%` stationary samples, so the run was mostly continuous with a few interruptions.

- Strava logged about `124.0 m` of climbing, with altitude moving between roughly `37.4 m` and `63.0 m`.
- Estimated climbing was `54.6 m` in the first half and `69.4 m` in the second, so most of the climbing landed later.
